17	// DefaultReviewMaxRetries is the number of reviewer attempts allowed per
18	// committed result_rev before the loop emits a terminal
19	// `review-manual-required` event and stops re-executing primary. FEAT-022 §14.
20	const DefaultReviewMaxRetries = 3
21	
22	type ExecuteBeadReport struct {
23		BeadID      string `json:"bead_id"`
24		AttemptID   string `json:"attempt_id,omitempty"`
25		WorkerID    string `json:"worker_id,omitempty"`
26		Harness     string `json:"harness,omitempty"`
27		Provider    string `json:"provider,omitempty"`
28		Model       string `json:"model,omitempty"`
29		Status      string `json:"status"`
30		Detail      string `json:"detail,omitempty"`
31		SessionID   string `json:"session_id,omitempty"`
32		BaseRev     string `json:"base_rev,omitempty"`
33		ResultRev   string `json:"result_rev,omitempty"`
34		PreserveRef string `json:"preserve_ref,omitempty"`
35		RetryAfter  string `json:"retry_after,omitempty"`
36		// NoChangesRationale carries the agent's explanation when status == no_changes.
37		NoChangesRationale string `json:"no_changes_rationale,omitempty"`
38		// ReviewVerdict is the post-merge review verdict (APPROVE, REQUEST_CHANGES,
39		// or BLOCK) when a reviewer ran. Empty when review was skipped.
40		ReviewVerdict string `json:"review_verdict,omitempty"`
41		// ReviewRationale carries the actionable reviewer-authored findings for
42		// non-APPROVE review outcomes.
43		ReviewRationale string `json:"review_rationale,omitempty"`
44		// Tier is the model tier used for the final attempt (cheap, standard, smart).
45		// Populated by tier-escalating executors; empty for single-tier attempts.
46		Tier string `json:"tier,omitempty"`
47		// ProbeResult is a brief summary of the provider health probe at attempt time.
48		ProbeResult string `json:"probe_result,omitempty"`
49		// CostUSD is the dollar cost of this attempt as reported by the harness.
50		// Tier-escalating executors propagate this so the escalation trace can
51		// compute wasted/effective spend.
52		CostUSD float64 `json:"cost_usd,omitempty"`
53		// DurationMS is the wall-clock duration of this attempt.
54		DurationMS int64 `json:"duration_ms,omitempty"`
55		// Profile routing telemetry. Populated when execute-loop uses a profile
56		// ladder rather than an explicit harness/model pin.
57		RequestedProfile string `json:"requested_profile,omitempty"`
58		RequestedTier    string `json:"requested_tier,omitempty"`
59		ResolvedTier     string `json:"resolved_tier,omitempty"`
60		EscalationCount  int    `json:"escalation_count,omitempty"`
61		FinalTier        string `json:"final_tier,omitempty"`
62	}

64	type ExecuteBeadExecutor interface {
65		Execute(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error)
66	}
67	
68	type ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error)
69	
70	func (f ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc) Execute(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
71		return f(ctx, beadID)
72	}

74	// SatisfactionChecker evaluates whether a bead that returned no_changes is
75	// already satisfied and should be closed, or is still unresolved and should
76	// receive retry suppression. noChangesCount is the cumulative count including
77	// the current attempt.
78	//
79	// When satisfied is true the caller closes the bead with the returned evidence
80	// string as the detail. When false the caller applies a retry cooldown and
81	// leaves the bead open.
82	type SatisfactionChecker interface {
83		CheckSatisfied(ctx context.Context, beadID string, noChangesCount int) (satisfied bool, evidence string, err error)
84	}
85	
86	// SatisfactionCheckerFunc is a functional adapter for SatisfactionChecker.
87	type SatisfactionCheckerFunc func(ctx context.Context, beadID string, noChangesCount int) (bool, string, error)
88	
89	func (f SatisfactionCheckerFunc) CheckSatisfied(ctx context.Context, beadID string, noChangesCount int) (bool, string, error) {
90		return f(ctx, beadID, noChangesCount)
91	}
92	
93	type ExecuteBeadLoopStore interface {
94		ReadyExecution() ([]bead.Bead, error)
95		Claim(id, assignee string) error
96		Unclaim(id string) error
97		Heartbeat(id string) error
98		CloseWithEvidence(id, sessionID, commitSHA string) error
99		AppendEvent(id string, event bead.BeadEvent) error
100		Events(id string) ([]bead.BeadEvent, error)
101		SetExecutionCooldown(id string, until time.Time, status, detail string) error
102		IncrNoChangesCount(id string) (int, error)
103		// Reopen sets a closed bead back to open, appending notes to the bead's
104		// Notes field and recording a reopen event. Used by the post-merge review
105		// step when the reviewer returns REQUEST_CHANGES or BLOCK.
106		Reopen(id, reason, notes string) error
107	}
108	
109	// readyDiagnoser is the optional interface the work loop uses to explain an
110	// empty execution queue. bead.Store satisfies it via ReadyExecutionBreakdown.
111	type readyDiagnoser interface {
112		ReadyExecutionBreakdown() (bead.ReadyExecutionBreakdown, error)
113	}
114	
115	// NoReadyWorkBreakdown explains why the execution-ready queue is empty when
116	// dependency-ready beads exist. Populated on an ExecuteBeadLoopResult when
117	// NoReadyWork fires and the store exposes ReadyExecutionBreakdown.
118	type NoReadyWorkBreakdown struct {
119		SkippedEpics       []string `json:"skipped_epics,omitempty"`
120		SkippedOnCooldown  []string `json:"skipped_on_cooldown,omitempty"`
121		SkippedNotEligible []string `json:"skipped_not_eligible,omitempty"`
122		SkippedSuperseded  []string `json:"skipped_superseded,omitempty"`
123		NextRetryAfter     string   `json:"next_retry_after,omitempty"`
124	}
125	
126	// ProgressEvent is the FEAT-006 structured progress event. It is defined
127	// separately in the server package (server.ProgressEvent); this alias lets
128	// the agent package emit events without importing the server package.
129	// The field names and types are identical — the server package deserialises
130	// these directly from the channel.
131	//
132	// Terminal phases: done, preserved, failed.
133	type ProgressEvent struct {
134		EventID   string    `json:"event_id"`
135		AttemptID string    `json:"attempt_id"`
136		WorkerID  string    `json:"worker_id"`
137		ProjectID string    `json:"project_id"`
138		BeadID    string    `json:"bead_id"`
139		Harness   string    `json:"harness,omitempty"`
140		Model     string    `json:"model,omitempty"`
141		Profile   string    `json:"profile,omitempty"`
142		Phase     string    `json:"phase"`
143		PhaseSeq  int       `json:"phase_seq"`
144		Heartbeat bool      `json:"heartbeat"`
145		TS        time.Time `json:"ts"`
146		ElapsedMS int64     `json:"elapsed_ms"`
147		Message   string    `json:"message,omitempty"`
148	}
149	
150	type ExecuteBeadLoopOptions struct {
151		Assignee                string
152		Once                    bool
153		PollInterval            time.Duration
154		NoProgressCooldown      time.Duration
155		MaxNoChangesBeforeClose int
156		// HeartbeatInterval, if > 0, overrides bead.HeartbeatInterval for this
157		// worker's claim heartbeat loop. Tests use this to shorten the tick.
158		HeartbeatInterval time.Duration
159		Log               io.Writer
160		// NoReview, when true, skips the post-merge review step even when
161		// ExecuteBeadWorker.Reviewer is configured. Use for doc-only beads or
162		// tight iteration loops where review latency is not acceptable.
163		NoReview bool
164	
165		// ReviewMaxRetries caps the number of reviewer attempts per committed
166		// result_rev before the loop emits a terminal `review-manual-required`
167		// event and parks the bead (FEAT-022 §14). Zero or negative falls back
168		// to DefaultReviewMaxRetries (3). The counter is scoped to a single
169		// result_rev: a fresh result_rev resets the counter, and iterations
170		// without a committed result_rev (--no-merge, execution-failed) do not
171		// consume the budget because the reviewer is not invoked.
172		ReviewMaxRetries int
173	
174		// EventSink receives structured JSONL progress events emitted at
175		// loop.start, bead.claimed, bead.result, and loop.end milestones.
176		// When nil, no structured events are written. Log (terminal text)
177		// is independent and still emitted for human operators.
178		EventSink io.Writer
179	
180		// ProgressCh, when non-nil, receives FEAT-006 ProgressEvents for each
181		// bead execution managed by this loop. The caller is responsible for
182		// draining the channel; the loop sends non-blocking (events are dropped
183		// if the channel is full). The loop does NOT close this channel; the
184		// caller (WorkerManager.runWorker) closes it after Run returns.
185		ProgressCh chan<- ProgressEvent
186	
187		// PreClaimHook, when non-nil, is called before Store.Claim for each
188		// candidate bead. If it returns an error the bead is not claimed and the
189		// loop continues to the next iteration (ctx is NOT cancelled). A nil hook
190		// disables the check. Server and CLI paths wire a real implementation
191		// backed by LandingGitOps.FetchOriginAncestryCheck; tests may inject nil
192		// or a stub that always returns nil.
193		PreClaimHook func(ctx context.Context) error
194	
195		// Worker/session metadata included in loop.start events so log
196		// aggregators can correlate structured output with the executing
197		// harness/worker. None of these are required.
198		WorkerID    string
199		ProjectRoot string
200		Harness     string
201		Model       string
202		Profile     string
203		Provider    string
204		ModelRef    string
205		SessionID   string
206		LabelFilter string
207	
208		// MinTier and MaxTier bound the tier escalation range when the executor
209		// uses tier-based auto-escalation. Empty string uses the defaults (cheap
210		// and smart). Ignored when the executor has escalation disabled (e.g. an
211		// explicit --harness or --model override was specified).
212		MinTier string
213		MaxTier string
214	}
215	
216	type ExecuteBeadLoopResult struct {
217		Attempts          int                  `json:"attempts"`
218		Successes         int                  `json:"successes"`
219		Failures          int                  `json:"failures"`
220		NoReadyWork       bool                 `json:"no_ready_work,omitempty"`
221		NoReadyWorkDetail NoReadyWorkBreakdown `json:"no_ready_work_detail,omitempty"`
222		LastSuccessAt     time.Time            `json:"last_success_at,omitempty"`
223		LastFailureStatus string               `json:"last_failure_status,omitempty"`
224		Results           []ExecuteBeadReport  `json:"results,omitempty"`
225	}
226	
227	// ExecuteBeadWorker drains the current single-project execution-ready queue.
228	// It intentionally does not retry a failed/conflicted bead again in the same
229	// process run; a later operator-driven invocation can create the next attempt.
230	type ExecuteBeadWorker struct {
231		Store               ExecuteBeadLoopStore
232		Executor            ExecuteBeadExecutor
233		SatisfactionChecker SatisfactionChecker // nil → count-based default
234		Now                 func() time.Time
235		// Reviewer, when non-nil, is called after every successful merge to
236		// validate the commit against the bead's acceptance criteria. When nil,
237		// post-merge review is skipped (same behaviour as --no-review).
238		Reviewer BeadReviewer
239	}
240	
241	// emitProgress sends a ProgressEvent to opts.ProgressCh non-blocking.
242	// If ch is nil or full the event is silently dropped.
243	func emitProgress(ch chan<- ProgressEvent, evt ProgressEvent) {
244		if ch == nil {
245			return
246		}
247		select {
248		case ch <- evt:
249		default:
250		}
251	}
252	
253	// newProgressEvent builds a ProgressEvent with a random event_id and current timestamp.
254	func newProgressEvent(workerID, projectID, beadID, attemptID, phase string, phaseSeq int, heartbeat bool, elapsedMS int64, opts ExecuteBeadLoopOptions) ProgressEvent {
255		return ProgressEvent{
256			EventID:   "evt-" + randomProgressID(),
257			AttemptID: attemptID,
258			WorkerID:  workerID,
259			ProjectID: projectID,
260			BeadID:    beadID,
261			Harness:   opts.Harness,
262			Model:     opts.Model,
263			Profile:   opts.Profile,
264			Phase:     phase,
265			PhaseSeq:  phaseSeq,
266			Heartbeat: heartbeat,
267			TS:        time.Now().UTC(),
268			ElapsedMS: elapsedMS,
269		}
270	}
271	
272	func randomProgressID() string {
273		return fmt.Sprintf("%x", time.Now().UnixNano())[:8]
274	}
275	
276	func (w *ExecuteBeadWorker) Run(ctx context.Context, opts ExecuteBeadLoopOptions) (*ExecuteBeadLoopResult, error) {
277		if w.Store == nil {
278			return nil, fmt.Errorf("execute-bead loop: store is required")
279		}
280		if w.Executor == nil {
281			return nil, fmt.Errorf("execute-bead loop: executor is required")
282		}
283	
284		now := w.Now
285		if now == nil {
286			now = time.Now
287		}
288		assignee := opts.Assignee
289		if assignee == "" {
290			assignee = "ddx"
291		}
292		noProgressCooldown := opts.NoProgressCooldown
293		if noProgressCooldown <= 0 {
294			noProgressCooldown = 6 * time.Hour
295		}
296		maxNoChangesBeforeClose := opts.MaxNoChangesBeforeClose
297		if maxNoChangesBeforeClose <= 0 {
298			maxNoChangesBeforeClose = 3
299		}
300		heartbeatInterval := opts.HeartbeatInterval
301		if heartbeatInterval <= 0 {
302			heartbeatInterval = bead.HeartbeatInterval
303		}
304	
305		result := &ExecuteBeadLoopResult{}
306		attempted := make(map[string]struct{})
307	
308		emit := func(eventType string, data map[string]any) {
309			writeLoopEvent(opts.EventSink, opts.SessionID, eventType, data, now().UTC())
310		}
311	
312		emit("loop.start", map[string]any{
313			"worker_id":    opts.WorkerID,
314			"project_root": opts.ProjectRoot,
315			"harness":      opts.Harness,
316			"model":        opts.Model,
317			"session_id":   opts.SessionID,
318			"assignee":     assignee,
319			"once":         opts.Once,
320		})
321		defer func() {
322			emit("loop.end", map[string]any{
323				"attempts":            result.Attempts,
324				"successes":           result.Successes,
325				"failures":            result.Failures,
326				"last_failure_status": result.LastFailureStatus,
327			})
328		}()
329	
330		for {
331			// Respect context cancellation between iterations. Without this check,
332			// a Stop() request (which cancels ctx) would only take effect during
333			// the idle poll sleep — the loop would happily claim the next ready
334			// bead as soon as the current Execute returned, ignoring the cancel.
335			if err := ctx.Err(); err != nil {
336				return result, err
337			}
338	
339			candidate, ok, err := w.nextCandidate(attempted, opts.LabelFilter)
340			if err != nil {
341				return result, err
342			}
343			if !ok {
344				if result.Attempts == 0 {
345					result.NoReadyWork = true
346					if diag, ok := w.Store.(readyDiagnoser); ok {
347						if breakdown, bErr := diag.ReadyExecutionBreakdown(); bErr == nil {
348							result.NoReadyWorkDetail = NoReadyWorkBreakdown{
349								SkippedEpics:       breakdown.SkippedEpics,
350								SkippedOnCooldown:  breakdown.SkippedOnCooldown,
351								SkippedNotEligible: breakdown.SkippedNotEligible,
352								SkippedSuperseded:  breakdown.SkippedSuperseded,
353								NextRetryAfter:     breakdown.NextRetryAfter,
354							}
355						}
356					}
357				}
358				if opts.PollInterval <= 0 {
359					return result, nil
360				}
361				if err := sleepWithContext(ctx, opts.PollInterval); err != nil {
362					return result, err
363				}
364				continue
365			}
366	
367			attempted[candidate.ID] = struct{}{}
368	
369			// Pre-claim hook: fetch origin + verify ancestry before claiming.
370			// On error the bead is skipped for this iteration; the loop
371			// continues (ctx is not cancelled).
372			if opts.PreClaimHook != nil {
373				if hookErr := opts.PreClaimHook(ctx); hookErr != nil {
374					if opts.Log != nil {
375						_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(opts.Log, "pre-claim hook: %v (skipping %s)\n", hookErr, candidate.ID)
376					}
377					emit("preclaim.skipped", map[string]any{
378						"bead_id": candidate.ID,
379						"reason":  hookErr.Error(),
380					})
381					continue
382				}
383			}
384	
385			if err := w.Store.Claim(candidate.ID, assignee); err != nil {
386				continue
387			}
388	
389			emit("bead.claimed", map[string]any{
390				"bead_id":  candidate.ID,
391				"title":    candidate.Title,
392				"assignee": assignee,
393			})
394	
395			if opts.Log != nil {
396				if candidate.Title != "" {
397					_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(opts.Log, "\n▶ %s: %s\n", candidate.ID, candidate.Title)
398				} else {
399					_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(opts.Log, "\n▶ %s\n", candidate.ID)
400				}
401			}
402	
403			// Generate a provisional attempt_id for progress events.
404			// The real attempt_id is assigned inside ExecuteBead; we use this
405			// for queueing/running events and replace with the real one once known.
406			provAttemptID := time.Now().UTC().Format("20060102T150405") + "-" + randomProgressID()
407			runStart := now()
408			phaseSeq := 0
409			nextPhase := func(phase string, heartbeat bool) {
410				phaseSeq++
411				emitProgress(opts.ProgressCh, newProgressEvent(
412					opts.WorkerID, opts.ProjectRoot, candidate.ID, provAttemptID,
413					phase, phaseSeq, heartbeat, now().Sub(runStart).Milliseconds(), opts,
414				))
415			}
416	
417			nextPhase("queueing", false)
418	
419			hbCtx, hbCancel := context.WithCancel(ctx)
420			var hbWG sync.WaitGroup
421			hbWG.Add(1)
422			go func(beadID string) {
423				defer hbWG.Done()
424				ticker := time.NewTicker(heartbeatInterval)
425				defer ticker.Stop()
426				for {
427					select {
428					case <-hbCtx.Done():
429						return
430					case <-ticker.C:
431						_ = w.Store.Heartbeat(beadID)
432					}
433				}
434			}(candidate.ID)
435	
436			nextPhase("running", false)
437	
438			report, err := w.Executor.Execute(ctx, candidate.ID)
439			hbCancel()
440			hbWG.Wait()
441			if err != nil {
442				report = ExecuteBeadReport{
443					BeadID: candidate.ID,
444					Status: ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed,
445					Detail: err.Error(),
446				}
447			}
448			if report.BeadID == "" {
449				report.BeadID = candidate.ID
450			}
451			if report.Status == "" {
452				report.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed
453			}
454			if report.Detail == "" {
455				report.Detail = ExecuteBeadStatusDetail(report.Status, "", "")
456			}
457	
458			result.Attempts++
459	
460			if report.Status == ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess {
461				appendLoopRoutingEvidence(w.Store, candidate.ID, report, now().UTC())
462				// Close the bead early when review is skipped. The closure gate
463				// (ddx-e30e60a9) accepts this path because closing_commit_sha is
464				// set and there is no malformed-APPROVE event to reject.
465				reviewApproved := true
466				reviewSkipped := w.Reviewer == nil || opts.NoReview || HasBeadLabel(candidate.Labels, "review:skip")
467	
468				if reviewSkipped {
469					if err := w.Store.CloseWithEvidence(candidate.ID, report.SessionID, report.ResultRev); err != nil {
470						return result, err
471					}
472				}
473	
474				if w.Reviewer != nil && !opts.NoReview && !HasBeadLabel(candidate.Labels, "review:skip") {
475					reviewRes, reviewErr := w.Reviewer.ReviewBead(ctx, candidate.ID, report.ResultRev, report.Harness, report.Model)
476					if reviewErr != nil {
477						// FEAT-022 §12+§14: classify the failure into the four-class
478						// taxonomy and count prior review-error events scoped to the
479						// current result_rev. On the Nth failure emit a terminal
480						// review-manual-required event instead of yet another
481						// review-error, parking the bead so a subsequent loop
482						// iteration does NOT re-execute primary.
483						class := classifyReviewError(reviewErr, reviewRes)
484						prior := countPriorReviewErrors(w.Store, candidate.ID, report.ResultRev)
485						attemptCount := prior + 1
486						maxRetries := opts.ReviewMaxRetries
487						if maxRetries <= 0 {
488							maxRetries = DefaultReviewMaxRetries
489						}
490						errSummary := EventBodySummary{
491							InputBytes:  0,
492							OutputBytes: 0,
493						}
494						if reviewRes != nil {
495							errSummary = EventBodySummary{
496								Harness:     reviewRes.ReviewerHarness,
497								Model:       reviewRes.ReviewerModel,
498								InputBytes:  reviewRes.InputBytes,
499								OutputBytes: reviewRes.OutputBytes,
500								ElapsedMS:   reviewRes.DurationMS,
501							}
502						}
503						if attemptCount >= maxRetries {
504							body := appendEventSummary(reviewErrorEventBody(class, attemptCount, report.ResultRev, reviewErr.Error()), errSummary)
505							_ = w.Store.AppendEvent(candidate.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
506								Kind:      "review-manual-required",
507								Summary:   class,
508								Body:      body,
509								Actor:     assignee,
510								Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
511								CreatedAt: now().UTC(),
512							})
513							// Park the bead with a long cooldown so subsequent
514							// loop iterations do NOT re-pick it for primary
515							// re-execution. Reviewer-failure invariant (§13) is
516							// preserved: the bead is NOT closed.
517							parkUntil := now().UTC().Add(365 * 24 * time.Hour)
518							_ = w.Store.SetExecutionCooldown(candidate.ID, parkUntil, "review-manual-required", class)
519						} else {
520							body := appendEventSummary(reviewErrorEventBody(class, attemptCount, report.ResultRev, reviewErr.Error()), errSummary)
521							_ = w.Store.AppendEvent(candidate.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
522								Kind:      "review-error",
523								Summary:   class,
524								Body:      body,
525								Actor:     assignee,
526								Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
527								CreatedAt: now().UTC(),
528							})
529						}
530					} else {
531						report.ReviewVerdict = string(reviewRes.Verdict)
532						report.ReviewRationale = reviewRes.Rationale
533						// Persist the full reviewer stream as an artifact so the

679						result.LastFailureStatus = report.Status

681				} else {
682					if shouldSuppressNoProgress(report) {
683						retryAfter := now().UTC().Add(noProgressCooldown)

685							return result, err
686						}

694			result.Results = append(result.Results, report)

723				ProjectID: opts.ProjectRoot,
724				BeadID:    candidate.ID,
725				Harness:   opts.Harness,
726				Model:     opts.Model,
727				Profile:   opts.Profile,
728				Phase:     terminalPhase,
729				PhaseSeq:  phaseSeq,
730				Heartbeat: false,
731				TS:        now().UTC(),
732				ElapsedMS: now().Sub(runStart).Milliseconds(),
733				Message:   report.Detail,
734			})
735	
736			emit("bead.result", map[string]any{
737				"bead_id":              candidate.ID,
738				"status":               report.Status,
739				"detail":               report.Detail,
740				"session_id":           report.SessionID,
741				"result_rev":           report.ResultRev,
742				"base_rev":             report.BaseRev,
743				"preserve_ref":         report.PreserveRef,
744				"no_changes_rationale": report.NoChangesRationale,

964	// classifyReviewError maps a reviewer error and partial result into one of the
965	// four FEAT-022 §12 taxonomy classes. Resolution order:
966	//  1. reviewRes.Error if it's already one of the canonical class identifiers
967	//     (the reviewer set it explicitly — preferred path).
968	//  2. The error string text (for backwards-compatible reviewers that only
969	//     embed the class in their message).
970	//  3. ErrReviewVerdictUnparseable as a fallback when the strict-parse error
971	//     leaks through a reviewer that did not set Error.
972	//  4. Default to transport (network/provider-side failure) when nothing else
973	//     matches.
974	func classifyReviewError(reviewErr error, reviewRes *ReviewResult) string {
975		if reviewRes != nil {
976			switch reviewRes.Error {
977			case evidence.OutcomeReviewContextOverflow,
978				evidence.OutcomeReviewProviderEmpty,
979				evidence.OutcomeReviewUnparseable,
980				evidence.OutcomeReviewTransport:
981				return reviewRes.Error
982			}
983		}
984		msg := ""
985		if reviewErr != nil {
986			msg = reviewErr.Error()
987		}
988		switch {
989		case strings.Contains(msg, evidence.OutcomeReviewContextOverflow):
990			return evidence.OutcomeReviewContextOverflow
991		case strings.Contains(msg, evidence.OutcomeReviewProviderEmpty):
992			return evidence.OutcomeReviewProviderEmpty
993		case strings.Contains(msg, evidence.OutcomeReviewUnparseable):
994			return evidence.OutcomeReviewUnparseable
995		case strings.Contains(msg, evidence.OutcomeReviewTransport):
996			return evidence.OutcomeReviewTransport
997		case reviewErr != nil && strings.Contains(msg, ErrReviewVerdictUnparseable.Error()):
998			return evidence.OutcomeReviewUnparseable
999		}
1000		return evidence.OutcomeReviewTransport
1001	}

honored ExecuteBeadLoopOptions.ReviewMaxRetries, but no caller in cmd/
read the resolved value, leaving the YAML override (review_max_retries)
inert end-to-end. Pass cfg.ResolveReviewMaxRetries() into the options
struct so FEAT-022 §14 retry-cap overrides reach the loop.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>"
